title: Search is really slow
body: The search feature used to be fast but now it takes forever. Sometimes it takes 10-15 seconds to get results. I have a lot of tasks but this seems excessive. Running TaskFlow on my work laptop.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — slow search is definitely frustrating, especially when it used to work well.

Can you tell me a bit more about what changed? You mentioned it used to be fast — do you remember roughly when you first noticed the slowdown? For example, was it after a TaskFlow update, after your task list grew past a certain size, or did it seem to happen suddenly one day? Any context around what was different "before" versus "now" would really help narrow this down.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I think it started maybe 2 weeks ago? I'm not 100% sure but I believe I updated TaskFlow around that time — went from 2.2 to 2.3 I think. It wasn't a gradual thing, it felt like it just got slow pretty suddenly. My task list has been growing over the past couple years but I've had a lot of tasks for a while and it was fine before.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the timing around the 2.2 → 2.3 upgrade is a strong clue. I'd love to understand the slowdown a bit better from your side.

When you search, is it slow every time regardless of what you type, or are there certain kinds of searches that are worse than others? For instance, does searching for a single common word feel different from searching for something very specific? And does it feel like the delay is before *any* results start appearing, or do partial results show up quickly and then it hangs while loading more?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Hmm, now that you mention it, I think searching by task title is actually still pretty fast. It's when I search across the full descriptions that it gets really slow. I have some tasks with pretty long descriptions — like I paste meeting notes into them sometimes. The delay is before any results show up at all, it just hangs for a while and then everything appears at once.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks, that distinction between title search and description search is a really useful clue — it narrows things down a lot.

Let me make sure I'm understanding the full picture: after upgrading from TaskFlow 2.2 to 2.3 about two weeks ago, searching across task descriptions became very slow (10-15 seconds with no partial results), while title-only search still feels fast. You have a large, long-lived task list with some tasks containing lengthy descriptions like pasted meeting notes. Does that sound right?

One more thing that would help a developer reproduce this: roughly how many tasks are we talking about? Hundreds? Thousands? Even a ballpark would be useful.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, that's a pretty accurate summary. I've got around 5,000 tasks built up over about 2 years. And yeah, some of those descriptions are pretty beefy — I tend to dump meeting notes in there, some of them are probably a couple thousand words. Title search is still snappy though, it's just the description search that crawls.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Full-text description search regressed to ~10-15s after upgrading to TaskFlow 2.3","problem":"After upgrading from TaskFlow 2.2 to 2.3 approximately two weeks ago, searching across task descriptions takes 10-15 seconds with no partial/incremental results. Title-only search remains fast. The user has ~5,000 tasks accumulated over 2 years, many containing lengthy descriptions (pasted meeting notes, some several thousand words).","root_cause_hypothesis":"The 2.3 release likely introduced a regression in full-text description search — possibly a dropped or changed index on the description field, a switch from indexed search to naive full-table scan, or a change in the search query path that bypasses previously-used indexing. The fact that title search is unaffected and the slowdown was sudden (not gradual with data growth) points to a code/schema change rather than a data volume issue.","reproduction_steps":["Create or use a TaskFlow instance with a large number of tasks (~5,000) including many with long description fields (1,000-2,000+ words)","Perform a search that targets task descriptions (full-text search, not title-only)","Observe that the search hangs for 10-15 seconds before returning all results at once","Compare with the same search on TaskFlow 2.2 to confirm the regression"],"environment":"TaskFlow 2.3 (upgraded from 2.2), running on a work laptop. ~5,000 tasks with some containing multi-thousand-word descriptions.","severity":"high","impact":"Any user with a non-trivial number of tasks performing full-text description search experiences severe slowdown. This is a core search workflow, and the 10-15 second delay makes it effectively unusable for iterative searching.","recommended_fix":"Diff the search implementation between 2.2 and 2.3, focusing on how description search is executed. Check for: (1) removed or altered database indexes on the description field, (2) changes to the search query (e.g., switching from indexed FTS to LIKE/ILIKE scans), (3) new serialization or processing of description content before search. Restore or add appropriate full-text indexing on the description column.","proposed_test_case":"Performance test: seed a database with 5,000 tasks (500 of which have descriptions over 1,000 words), execute a full-text description search, and assert that results return within an acceptable threshold (e.g., under 2 seconds). This test should run against both title and description search paths to catch future regressions in either.","information_gaps":["Exact TaskFlow 2.3 build/patch version (reporter said 'I think' re: version numbers)","Whether other users on 2.3 with large datasets experience the same issue","Database backend in use (SQLite vs PostgreSQL vs other) — may affect indexing behavior"]}
